issues.description: create_solution_map has zero tests, while the functionally identical build_solved_values has four dedicated unit tests covering: normal multi-param construction, empty params returning an empty map, dimensionless param types, and a #[should_panic] test for the length-mismatch assert. The new function introduces an assert_eq! that will panic at runtime on mismatched slice lengths — the exact failure mode covered by build_solved_values's 'panics_on_length_mismatch' test — but that branch is completely untested here.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a parallel test module for create_solution_map mirroring the four existing build_solved_values tests: (1) correct HashMap construction with mixed dimension types, (2) empty slices return empty map, (3) dimensionless param type, (4) #[should_panic] for differing slice lengths. If create_solution_map is intended to replace build_solved_values, migrate and rename the existing tests instead of duplicating them.

issues.description: create_solution_map is a line-for-line duplicate of build_solved_values (same assert, same zip/map/collect, same Value::Scalar construction). The diff does not include any call site for create_solution_map, so it is currently dead code. Untested dead code carries no coverage value and risks diverging silently from build_solved_values if either is changed later.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) delete create_solution_map and consolidate on build_solved_values with its existing tests, or (b) include the call sites in this diff and add tests that exercise create_solution_map through those paths so coverage is meaningful.

issues.description: Neither the new function nor its tests (absent here) cover a param whose param_type is not Type::Scalar — dimension_of returns DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS for non-Scalar types. build_solved_values has a 'dimensionless_type' test covering this branch. create_solution_map omits it entirely.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a test that passes an AutoParam whose param_type is not Type::Scalar and asserts the resulting Value::Scalar carries DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS, matching the coverage already present for build_solved_values.

sonnet_test_analyst.summary: The diff introduces create_solution_map, which is a functionally identical duplicate of the existing build_solved_values. The critical problem from a test-coverage standpoint is that build_solved_values ships with four unit tests (normal construction, empty input, dimensionless type, and a #[should_panic] for the length-mismatch assert_eq!), while create_solution_map ships with none. The assert_eq! panic path is the most dangerous gap: it is an explicit runtime failure mode that is tested on the original function but completely untested on the new one. Additionally, no call site for create_solution_map appears in the diff, making it dead code — untested dead code provides no coverage value and is liable to drift silently. The blocking issue is the absent tests for new code that duplicates already-tested logic; the suggestions address the broader duplication concern and the missing dimensionless-type edge-case test.

issues.description: `create_solution_map` is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase. As a private `fn` with no callers, the Rust compiler will emit a `dead_code` warning. If the crate (or workspace) has `#![deny(warnings)]` or an equivalent lint configuration—common in production Rust projects—this will be a compile error. Even without that, the diff is incomplete: a function added with no callsite means the intended refactor or new code path was not wired up.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) add the callsite(s) that replace the existing invocations of `build_solved_values` with `create_solution_map`, or (b) remove the new function entirely. If this function is truly meant to replace `build_solved_values`, migrate all four call-sites in solver.rs (lines ~537, ~662, ~697, and the test helpers) and delete the original.

issues.description: `create_solution_map` is functionally identical to the existing `build_solved_values` (same `assert_eq!` guard, same `zip`/`map`/`collect` pipeline, same `Value::Scalar` construction via `dimension_of`). The only differences are the parameter names. Maintaining two copies of this logic is a maintenance liability: future changes to the mapping (e.g. handling non-Scalar types, changing the dimension fallback) must be applied twice.
issues.suggested_fix: Consolidate to a single function. If the new naming (`specifications`/`solution_values`) is preferred for clarity, rename the existing `build_solved_values` and its call-sites instead of introducing a duplicate.

sonnet_bug_hunter.summary: The diff introduces `create_solution_map`, a private helper that is semantically identical to the pre-existing `build_solved_values` but is never called anywhere in the codebase. This is dead code that will produce at minimum a compiler warning and, if the workspace enforces `deny(warnings)`, a compile failure. The diff appears to be an incomplete refactor: the new function was defined but its call-sites were not wired up (or `build_solved_values` was not removed). No new logic errors are introduced—the assert/zip/map pattern is correct and consistent with the existing function—but the incomplete state of the change is a blocking concern.

issues.description: create_solution_map is a near-exact duplicate of the pre-existing build_solved_values function (lines 55–74). Both accept (&[AutoParam], &[f64]), assert equal length, zip-and-map into HashMap<ValueCellId, Value::Scalar { si_value, dimension }>, and return the same type. The only differences are parameter names and assert message wording. The existing function's own doc comment already explicitly states it is 'Used by early-exit, fallback, and solution construction paths', indicating it was designed for general reuse. Introducing a second function with identical semantics violates DRY, creates confusion about which to call, and risks silent divergence if one is updated without the other.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete create_solution_map entirely. Call build_solved_values at every site where create_solution_map was intended to be used. If the longer parameter names were meant to serve as documentation, they can instead be noted in a comment at the call site or by renaming build_solved_values itself (e.g. to build_solution_map) in a single controlled rename rather than duplicating it.

sonnet_design_critic.summary: The diff introduces create_solution_map, a function that is functionally identical to the already-existing build_solved_values: same input types (&[AutoParam], &[f64]), same assert-equal-length guard, same zip-map-collect body producing HashMap<ValueCellId, Value::Scalar>. The only variation is cosmetic (parameter names, assert message text). build_solved_values already carries a doc comment noting it is intended for 'solution construction paths', so no new abstraction is needed. This duplication should be rejected and the caller should use build_solved_values directly.
